replies.author: @joanl.7543
replies.text: Don't get me going on this.  Few people seem to be able to keep subject and object pronouns straight; if they aren't using "me" as a subject they're using "I" as an object.  If these were people who didn't speak, teach, or write for a living I would not gripe, but it does bother me when I hear journalists, or people with degrees giving lectures, and they mess that up.  If you write or speak to others for your living you should learn that rule.  And it's not even close to being just a problem with the Left.  Loads of excellent conservatives do it, doubtless because they hear it often.   It's not conscious.

replies.author: @Cwin-ny6bp
replies.text: @lad@ladyloucksrtainly agree that it should be rooted out wherever, whether it’s in the schools, churches, or wherever else. I was just pointing out that public school teachers offend at a significantly higher rate than Catholic priests, and the church thing was blasted like crazy while the teacher thing was covered up and ignored. This is also coming from someone who isn’t a Catholic and who is fairly critical of the Catholic Church forbidding priests from marrying, which I think made it more attractive for a particular kind of predator. I also say this as someone who assumes based on stats and experience that the vast majority of Catholic Priests aren’t predators and the ones that violate their vows are probably significantly more prone to doing so with adult women (which I honestly can’t fault too much).

author: @MrsCheb
text:
I don’t really care for the school news. You seem to get information wrong. Like you said she’s a college professor then said she’s a high school teacher. 
The kids these days are absolutely awful. My entire family are teachers in deep red state. My parents teachers for 40 years and they’re retiring this year. I too help out at school events. There are good kids, but the awful ones overpower. My poor dad who loved his job now hates it because he has to deal with the complication of his trans students, his neurologically challenged students, his hyperactive students. My brother had to go to court  because he broke up an extremely violent attack between students. My mom’s been called every name in the book. The kids are NOT alright Jeremy.

replies.author: @RJ_Tablet
replies.text: So it's all the kid's fault?

replies.author: @MrsCheb
replies.text:
@RJ_Tablet I’m pointing out that Jeremy seems to pull these sorts of stories to attack a certain field. No im not saying the conviction of this woman is the fault of the boy. I’m calling Jeremy out for being so biased. Inciting  that the majority of teachers are like the few crazies Jeremy pulls from the pot. It’s the fault of societal degradation within our country. My beef has been with the universities, not the public k-12 schools. These types of people are graduating into every field and also becoming parents. It’s just getting old hearing people gripe on teachers when they’re first on the front lines of dealing with these lunatics to begin with. It’s the introduction to social media and the social takeover of the universities that seem to contribute to the woke shift and the entitled people from my generation.
Just think about the millions of schools in our country that aren’t like this… it seems like constructed outrage for his clicks.

replies.author: @RJ_Tablet
replies.text: @MrsCheb I get what where you're comming from, thanks for explaining.

replies.author: @MrsCheb
replies.text: @RJ_Tablet I’m sorry for ranting. I don’t mean to be like that - and thanks
